public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant.
appearance: Typically appears in the bright colors and tall plumes associated with Shelk’s Roadwardens.
manner: Keeps speech clipped and threshold-focused, reading every arrival for risk, weakness, or breach. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of ritual attendant in and around Roadwardens’ Central Command, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Keep Roadwardens’ Central Command workable through the obligations of ritual attendant while managing the immediate pressure of institutional scrutiny in Transpolity.
hidden_pressure: Must keep visible order credible while absorbing the first shock of disorder, contraband, sabotage, or violence. Around Roadwardens’ Central Command, that usually means carrying institutional scrutiny, reputation risk, daily strain without letting the role of ritual attendant visibly slip.

adventure_hooks.summary: Taren Wren needs outside hands to handle a breach, seizure, or violent disturbance before it spreads through Roadwardens’ Central Command.
Taren Wren can draw the party into contraband, false papers, or a suspicious cargo problem tied to Roadwardens’ Central Command.

public_signal: Uniform, posture, and the way others make room mark Taren Wren as visible enforcement before rank ever needs to be named.

first_impression: Taren Wren presents as Keeps speech clipped and threshold-focused, reading every arrival for risk, weakness, or breach. In Transpolity, that reads as someone boundary-crossing and adaptive, speaking to whichever audience or protocol is presently in front of them, and the habits of a ritual attendant still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Ritual Attendant, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Transpolity.
what_they_refuse: Taren Wren refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Ritual Attendant too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Taren Wren can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Taren Wren adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Taren Wren is Known locally through day-to-day work as ritual attendant. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Ritual Attendant.
